item.title: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen drops reelection bid after redistricting
item.description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen of Tennessee announced Friday that he's retiring from Congress at the end of this term after his district in Memphis was redrawn.

item.title: Iran says it "cannot trust" U.S. in negotiations as Trump says "we control" strait
item.description: Speaking to reporters on Air Force One, President Trump also claimed to have "wiped out" Iran's armed forces.

item.title: What are the origins of hantavirus? How the virus tied to the cruise ship outbreak came to be.
item.description: The deadly hantavirus outbreak on a Dutch-flagged cruise ship was caused by the Andes virus, the only known strain to spread from person to person. Rodents are known to carry hantavirus, but what's the disease's origin story? Ramy Inocencio explains.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 17): "By Design"
item.description: "Sunday Morning" presents its annual edition touching on all aspects of design, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo kills dozens, Africa's CDC says
item.description: A new Ebola outbreak in a remote province in Congo has caused 65 deaths and 246 suspected cases so far, Africa CDC says.

item.title: What are the risks of trying to settle debt on your own?
item.description: A DIY approach to debt settlement can backfire if you don't understand the legal, financial and tax risks involved.

item.title: John Krasinski and Michael Kelly reunite for "Tom Clancy's Jack Ryan: Ghost War"
item.description: John Krasinski and Michael Kelly join "CBS Mornings" to discuss reuniting for the movie, "Tom Clancy's Jack Ryan: Ghost War," after starring in the TV series "Tom Clancy's Jack Ryan" for four seasons. They discuss the movie, their friendship and how they balance it all with their busy family lives.

item.title: Why is Trump talking about action on Cuba and what could that look like?
item.description: Experts don't expect military action soon. But actual regime change is complicated.

item.title: How will Jerome Powell be remembered as he exits as Fed chair?
item.description: As Powell steps down after more than eight years leading the Federal Reserve, economists say he helped steer the U.S. through historic shocks but misread inflation.

item.title: New season of "Couples Therapy" dives into political differences
item.description: Dr. Orna Guralnik speaks to "CBS Mornings" about the new season of the docuseries "Couples Therapy," which features Guralnik counseling four new couples on how to tackle issues in their relationships, including major political differences and infidelity.

item.title: Latest news on Cuba's future following CIA director's visit
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visited Cuba as more details emerge about potential moves by the U.S. CBS News' Olivia Gazis report.

item.title: What did Trump accomplish in China?
item.description: President Trump is headed back to the U.S. after a summit with President Xi Jinping in China. CBS News' Anna Coren, Olivia Rinaldi and Aaron MacLean report.

item.title: Are adjustable-rate mortgages worth considering right now? Here's what experts say
item.description: An ARM may get you a lower rate, but it's not the right move for everyone in today's economic environment.

item.title: Supreme Court allows mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: Four years after overturning Roe v. Wade, the Supreme Court in a 7-2 decision upheld mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone for now, blocking a lower court ruling. Jan Crawford reports on the court's decision and Dr. Celine Gounder explains what could happen next.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that might just become essentials in your everyday life. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Coast Guard stops boats holding 3 tons of cocaine, opens fire on one
item.description: The crew of the Tahoma, a 270-foot Coast Guard cutter, made the interdictions about 90 miles off the coast of Cartagena, Colombia.

item.title: How a growing El Niño will affect 2026 hurricane season in Atlantic, Pacific
item.description: The last time an El Niño pattern occurred was in 2023, when the Eastern Pacific hurricane season produced 20 tropical systems.

item.title: Trump touts "fantastic trade deals" with Xi as Beijing summit ends
item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded their summit in Beijing on Friday with both countries looking to claim the visit as a win.

item.title: Supreme Court preserves mail access to mifepristone abortion pill
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing mail access to continue for the abortion pill mifepristone, ensuring that patients nationwide can obtain the treatment for now. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: Passenger on plane that crashed in Atlantic reflects on being rescued
item.description: Eleven people were rescued by an elite Air Force unit after an emergency forced the pilot to land the plane in the Atlantic Ocean. Cristian Benavides spoke to a passenger and the pilot about what happened.

item.title: U.S. moving to indict Cuba's Raúl Castro, sources say
item.description: The potential indictment — which must be approved by a grand jury — is expected to focus on Cuba's 1996 downing of two planes operated by a humanitarian group.

item.title: Oklahoma becomes latest state to sue Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Oklahoma has filed a lawsuit against Roblox, making it the latest state to take legal action against the popular gaming platform over child safety concerns.

item.title: 2 senators call on FAA to study impact of reduced flight attendant staffing
item.description: Sens. Tammy Duckworth​ of Illinois and Tammy Baldwin of Wisconsin say their concern is there may be more emergency exit doors than flight attendants in the event of an evacuation.

item.title: RFK Jr. says he won't run for president in 2028
item.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is trying to address the interests of his MAHA supporters, who view him as their hope for the future, while being a good soldier in the eyes of the Trump White House.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Preakness Stakes
item.description: The Preakness Stakes will feature its biggest field in 15 years with 14 horses in the middle jewel of horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: FDA blocked melanoma drug as confusion reigned under Makary
item.description: A third of patients in a clinical trial had tumors shrink while taking a genetically engineered treatment known as RP1.

item.title: Why are so many U.S. CEOs in China with Trump, and what do they want?
item.description: More than a dozen American CEOs are accompanying President Trump on his trip to China. That's not unusual.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds mail access to abortion pill mifepristone for now
item.description: The Supreme Court has maintained mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone, setting aside for now a lower court order that blocked abortion providers from prescribing the widely used drug through telehealth and shipping it to patients.

item.title: Trump Mobile says its T1 mobile phone to ship this week
item.description: Trump Mobile's $499 gold-toned phone has faced delays since it was unveiled in June 2025.

item.title: Behind the rise of fast food and how it shaped the U.S.
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," "CBS Mornings" goes behind the scenes for a taste of history and shows how fast food has become part of American culture.
